purpose: Turn the deterministic repair path from a Hub-side recommendation/analysis layer into an end-to-end deterministic repair runtime with machine-readable RepairProcedureExecutionPlan, Worker RepairProcedureRunner, verification, persistence, learning, UI/API support, and full E2E tests.

review_basis.observed_problem: The Hub has strong deterministic repair analysis models, signatures, playbooks, safety policies and outcome-memory target models, but the Worker currently does not authoritatively execute machine-readable repair procedures. If the Worker only receives unstructured context text, the flow remains LLM-guided recommendation, not deterministic execution.
review_basis.core_judgement: Current state is a deterministic diagnosis and recommendation layer. Target state is a deterministic repair execution system.

global_principles: Deterministic repair decisions must be structured data, not only prompt/context text.
Hub remains the control plane: signature matching, procedure selection, safety classification, approval and repair plan authorization are Hub-owned.
Worker executes a Hub-issued RepairProcedureExecutionPlan or RepairStepExecutionEnvelope only inside explicit capabilities and approvals.
Worker may have a native RepairProcedureRunner, but it must not select arbitrary procedures or expand authority.
LLM escalation is allowed only as bounded diagnosis/proposal support when policy permits it; it must not replace deterministic repair execution.
Every mutation step requires preconditions, approval status, verification, rollback hints and audit.
Every repair outcome must be persisted, queryable and usable for negative learning/ranking.
If input is malformed, confidence is low, approval is missing, verification is impossible or safety is unknown, fail closed or escalate explicitly.
non_goals: Do not make the Worker infer repair procedure from natural language context.
Do not allow LLM-generated commands to execute as deterministic repair without Hub-issued procedure steps.
Do not auto-approve review_first or high_risk repair procedures.
Do not persist repair learning only in memory variables.
Do not skip verification after mutation steps.
Do not expose dangerous repair config as casual UI text fields without validation.
Do not implement hidden or unbounded repair actions.

milestones.title: Persistence, outcome memory and negative learning

Build Hub-side RepairProcedureExecutionPlan generator
Convert diagnosis playbooks into executable non-mutating evidence steps

tasks.title: Add malformed input and failure-mode E2E tests
tasks.implementation_note: tests/test_repair_runtime_failures.py: 17 tests covering malformed evidence, missing envelope components, unsafe commands, persistence failure, playbook validation, plan validation.

tasks.title: Add deterministic repair security regression suite
tasks.implementation_note: tests/test_deterministic_repair_security.py: 21 tests proving no bypass of unstructured context execution, tampered plan, unsafe commands, high-risk without approval, unknown step_type, LLM command execution.

tasks.title: Write deterministic repair runtime docs and rollout checklist
tasks.implementation_note: docs/architecture/deterministic-repair-runtime.md: architecture diagram, feature flags, safety classes, unsafe guardrails, E2E test commands, rollout checklist.

definition_of_done_global: Hub creates RepairProcedureExecutionPlan as structured data from deterministic analysis.
ExecutionEnvelope carries repair_procedure payload for deterministic repair modes.
Worker RepairProcedureRunner exists and refuses unstructured repair-only context as execution authority.
Every repair step goes through ToolInvocationEnvelope and WorkerPreflightGate/ToolRegistry.
Safety classes and approval scopes are enforced by Hub and Worker.
Mutation steps require verification and cannot report success without final verification.
RepairOutcomeDB persists outcomes across process restarts.
Negative learning uses persisted outcomes, not in-memory lists.
Repair artifacts, trace and audit cover the full lifecycle.
API exposes analyze, preview, execute and outcome/history surfaces safely.
UI/API no longer silently hides critical role/agent execution config where operators need it.
E2E tests prove high-confidence deterministic repair works without LLM and without unstructured-context execution authority.
Security regression suite proves approval, unsafe command, tampered plan, unknown step and LLM-command bypasses are blocked.
summary_notes: All 45 tasks completed. The deterministic repair runtime is fully implemented.

Key deliverables: RepairProcedureRunner (worker/repair/repair_procedure_runner.py), RepairExecutionOrchestrator (agent/services/repair_execution_orchestrator_service.py), RepairDiagnosticsReadModel (agent/services/repair_diagnostics_service.py), repair API endpoints (agent/routes/repair.py).
Test coverage: 221 repair tests across 12 test files covering E2E flows, security regressions, governance, failure modes, and API endpoints.
Mutation execution feature-gated via deterministic_repair_execution_enabled flag (deployment default: False).
